candidate: Bosch SMG180 OHW / Fiat 500e salvage SMG-family motor
status: reference spec only; Bosch direct is Tier 1/B2B, only currently tracked consumer path is salvage
key_questions: Do not assume consumer purchase from Bosch; validate Fiat 500e salvage variant, inverter pairing, resolver/control path, CAN/control docs, gearbox/differential interface, HV connectors, and coolant fittings

candidate: Nissan Leaf EM57 / complete Leaf drive unit
status: shortlisted practical budget candidate; strongest current DIY support
key_questions: Validate exact generation ZE0/AZE0/ZE1, matched inverter/motor set, Resolve or Thunderstruck VCU path, reverse RPM behavior, axle/IRS packaging, and compatibility with selected battery voltage

status: performance alternate; strong power but less mature open conversion path than Leaf
key_questions: Validate controller/inverter path, reduction/differential packaging, parking pawl behavior, HV connector/pinout, cooling, and whether support is mature enough for repeatable open-source builds

status: premium benchmark alternate; likely too expensive for first build
key_questions: Quote delivered cost, gearbox/final-drive package, VCU/control docs, CAD/manual access, and whether power can be de-rated for kei-truck use

status: turnkey premium alternate; useful benchmark but likely too expensive
cooling: WEG cooling system / supplied thermal package
weight: ~97.6 kg system weight for R300Si per Hypercraft
key_questions: Quote delivered cost, included VCU/harness/cooling, battery lock-in, CAD/manual package, and lower-power configuration options

status: lower-voltage DIY alternate; below current 300-430 V architecture
key_questions: Decide whether a lower-voltage architecture is acceptable; validate sustained hill/load performance, reduction gearing, controller current, and thermal limits

status: low-voltage lightweight prototype alternate; likely too weak for full reference truck
key_questions: Useful for mule/low-speed prototype only unless requirements are reduced; validate actual continuous power graph, controller voltage, and gearing

status: brushed DC prototype-only; not aligned with 300-430 V reference architecture
key_questions: Brushed DC remains possible but should not be reference architecture; validate brush maintenance, controller current, no/limited regen, overspeed protection, and high-current cabling

status: budget brushed DC prototype-only; not up to reference-truck target without reduced requirements
nominal_power_kw: ~7.5 continuous at 72 V from supplier text
peak_power_kw: ~46 with 120 V / 550 A controller per reseller
key_questions: Treat as low-speed/low-cost DC reference only; validate controller, current, heat, brush life, gearing, and vehicle mass limit
